subject: Physics
title: The Future of Nuclear Energy
description: A fast breakdown of The Future of Nuclear Energy for builders.

hook.text: Small modular reactors promise nuclear power that's cheaper, safer, and faster to build than traditional plants.

buildup.text: SMRs are factory-built, truck-sized reactors that can be shipped to remote locations.

discovery.text: Some designs use thorium or molten salt instead of uranium, producing far less long-lived waste.

twist.text: Nuclear power emits no carbon during operation, making it critical for fighting climate change.

climax.text: Countries are racing to approve SMRs. The first commercial units could operate by the late 2020s.

punchline.text: Smaller, safer, cleaner. Nuclear energy's next chapter.

quiz.question: What advantage do small modular reactors offer?
options: They are factory-built, cheaper, and faster to deploy
They use no fuel at all
They produce electricity from solar energy
